Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd, then known as Tian Jian Hua Xia Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd, was incorporated in Singapore on 2014-02-20 by Gong Ruizhong.
From 2014-02-20, Gong Ruizhong was a director of Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd and held that position throughout the period described.

As at the material times, at least 70.84% of the issued share capital of Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd was beneficially owned by Gong Ruizhong through his wholly-owned company Gongs Global Investment Development Holdings Limited.
On 2015-11-05, Tian Jian Hua Xia Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd was renamed Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd.
On 2017-06-12, OCA V Holdings Pte Ltd, a creditor of Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd, filed an application for a judicial management order in respect of Tendcare.
On 2017-07-28, the High Court allowed OCA V Holdings Pte Ltd's application to appoint Yit Chee Wah as interim judicial manager of Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd.
On 2017-09-11, the High Court placed Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd under judicial management and appointed Yit Chee Wah as judicial manager.
On 2014-06-09, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd received US$14,992,805.48 from EFG Atlantis China Pre-IPO Master Fund LP.
On 2014-06-09, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd received US$5,007,187.57 from Atlantis China Star Fund Limited.
On 2014-06-13, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd transferred US$5,000,000 to Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd.
On 2015-02-06, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd received US$49,987,532.13 from Chow Tai Fook Nominees Ltd in connection with an investment by Easom Limited.
On 2015-02-06, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd transferred US$15,000,000 to Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd.
On 2015-02-09, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd transferred US$5,000,000 to Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd.
On 2015-03-11, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd transferred US$2,000,000 to Tian Jian Hua Xia Medical Group (HK) Limited.
On 2015-02-27, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd transferred US$1,750,000 to Hui Xiang Group (HK) Limited.
On 2015-04-10, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd transferred US$2,540,000 to Hui Xiang Group (HK) Limited.
On 2015-09-16, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd received US$11,348,272.82 from OCA V Holdings Pte Ltd under a convertible note subscription arrangement.
On 2015-09-22, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd transferred US$4,000,000 to Tian Jian Hua Xia Medical Group (HK) Limited.
On 2015-09-23, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd transferred S$250,000 to Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd.
On 2015-09-25, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd transferred US$2,000,000 to Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd.
On 2015-10-26, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd transferred US$1,000,000 to Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd.
On 2015-11-12, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd received US$8,629,972.29 from Chow Tai Fook Nominees Ltd in connection with a further Easom Limited investment.
On 2015-11-13, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd transferred US$3,000,000 to Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd.
On 2015-11-16, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd transferred S$250,000 to Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd.
On 2015-12-08, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd transferred US$4,000,000 to Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd.

On 2015-03-31, Mari Mundi III Limited extended a US$40,000,000 loan to Tian Jian Hua Xia Medical Group (HK) Limited as pre-IPO financing, secured by charges over shares held by Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd and Gongs Global Investment Development Holdings Limited.
On 2015-09-10, OCA V Holdings Pte Ltd and Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd entered into a Convertible Note Subscription Agreement for a loan of US$19,978,280 to Tendcare.

On 2014-06-06, US$10,000 was paid by Sim Mong Teck to Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd as an initial payment under arrangements relating to NYC Investments Limited and Luxe Heritage Capital Management Limited.
On 2015-02-04, Easom Limited entered into a share purchase agreement to subscribe for 1,686 new ordinary shares in Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd at US$29,648.60 per share.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether pre-IPO funds raised for Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd and its group were subsequently transferred to external entities in a manner that affects the allocation of responsibility for the resulting debts and losses.
Party_Details.Rule: Where a company raises funds from investors or lenders for specified corporate purposes, persons who direct or participate in the use of those funds may be held personally responsible for the company’s debts if the business is carried on with intent to defraud creditors or for a fraudulent purpose, and they are knowingly involved in that conduct.
Party_Details.Application: Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd received substantial funds from equity investors and lenders between 2014 and 2015, including Atlantis China Star Fund Limited, EFG Atlantis China Pre-IPO Master Fund LP, Easom Limited, Mari Mundi III Limited, and OCA V Holdings Pte Ltd. Shortly after several of these receipts, Tendcare transferred significant amounts in US dollars and Singapore dollars to Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd, Tian Jian Hua Xia Medical Group (HK) Limited, and Hui Xiang Group (HK) Limited. These transfers form the transactional pattern that the court compared against the purposes for which the funds were raised, in order to determine whether any individuals or related entities should bear personal responsibility for Tendcare’s debts.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court analysed whether Tendcare’s business was carried on with intent to defraud creditors or for a fraudulent purpose under section 340(1) of the Companies Act, as applied via section 227X(b) in judicial management. It considered the plaintiffs’ theory of a scheme to use Tendcare as a vehicle to attract cash from investors under the pretext of an IPO and then divert funds through transfers to entities such as Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd, Qian Hui Capital Limited, and Hui Xiang Group (HK) Limited. The court rejected the contention that the NYC/Luxe share purchase agreement functioned as a fraudulent price floor influencing all investors, noting the independent role of KPMG and other advisers, the timing of Atlantis’ indicative offers, and the lack of evidence that lenders like MMIII and OCA relied on that agreement. The court nonetheless found that the pattern of transfers, the absence of credible evidence that the disputed sums were applied to the Tian Jian Group’s business or IPO expenses, and the lack of documentation tracing the funds supported an inference that Tendcare’s business was carried on for a fraudulent purpose. The judge held that Gong Ruizhong, as the controlling mind of Tendcare and Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd, authorised or caused the disputed transfers and failed to provide a satisfactory explanation or documentary trail, drawing an adverse inference against him. The court rejected the authenticity and probative value of the so-called Entrustment Agreement and concluded that Gong and Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd were knowingly parties to the fraudulent carrying on of Tendcare’s business and were personally responsible for specified debts. The court did not extend fraudulent trading liability to Miao Weiguo, Hui Xiang Group Pte Ltd, Hui Xiang Group (HK) Limited, or Qian Hui Capital Limited, finding insufficient proof that they participated in the overarching scheme, although it found that certain sham loan arrangements involving Qian Hui Capital Limited evidenced dishonesty relevant to other causes of action. The court also addressed claims for breach of directors’ duties, deceit, dishonest assistance, knowing receipt, conspiracy, unjust enrichment, and a counterclaim by Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd, resolving liability and quantum issues in light of its findings on the flow and use of funds.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Yit Chee Wah

Facts.Text: On 2017-07-28, the High Court appointed Yit Chee Wah as interim judicial manager of Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd.
On 2017-09-11, the High Court appointed Yit Chee Wah as judicial manager of Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd when the company was placed under judicial management.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the judicial manager of Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd can obtain a declaration that specific individuals and entities are personally responsible for Tendcare’s debts on the basis that its business was carried on with intent to defraud creditors or for a fraudulent purpose.
Party_Details.Rule: In judicial management, the court may apply the fraudulent trading provision so that, if it appears that any business of the company has been carried on with intent to defraud creditors or for any fraudulent purpose, any person who was knowingly a party to the carrying on of the business in that manner can be declared personally responsible, without limitation, for all or any of the company’s debts or liabilities.
Party_Details.Application: Acting as judicial manager, Yit Chee Wah sought relief under the fraudulent trading provision by pointing to the sequence of fund-raising transactions and subsequent transfers from Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd to external entities. The court examined these flows, the absence of credible evidence that the funds were used for the stated corporate purposes, and the roles of the individuals controlling the relevant companies. Based on this analysis, the court determined which parties were knowingly involved in the manner in which Tendcare’s business was carried on and identified the debts for which they should bear personal responsibility.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court accepted that the judicial manager was entitled to invoke the fraudulent trading mechanism in the context of judicial management by virtue of section 227X(b) of the Companies Act. It reviewed the evidence assembled by the judicial manager and his team, including bank records and transaction schedules, to assess whether Tendcare’s business had been carried on with intent to defraud creditors or for a fraudulent purpose. The judge concluded that the pattern of fund-raising and subsequent transfers, coupled with the lack of a credible explanation from the controlling director, satisfied the statutory threshold for fraudulent trading in relation to certain debts. The court therefore granted relief in favour of the judicial manager against specified defendants, while declining to extend fraudulent trading liability to others where the evidence did not show knowing participation in the fraudulent conduct.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Gong Ruizhong

Facts.Text: On 2014-02-20, Gong Ruizhong caused the incorporation of Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd, then named Tian Jian Hua Xia Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd, in Singapore and became its director.
From 2013-07-15, Gong Ruizhong was the sole director and shareholder of Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd, holding 2,500,001 fully paid-up shares.

On 2013-09-03, as legal representative and chairman of Beijing Tianjian Huaxia Medical Investment Management Co Ltd, Gong Ruizhong signed a memorandum of understanding with Hui Xiang Group Pte Ltd concerning a proposed initial public offering of Tendcare.

On 2013-09-03, a memorandum of understanding between Hui Xiang Group Pte Ltd and Beijing Tianjian Huaxia Medical Investment Management Co Ltd provided that Beijing Tianjian Huaxia Medical Investment Management Co Ltd would pay Hui Xiang Group Pte Ltd a monthly retainer of S$100,000, Hui Xiang Group Pte Ltd would receive a success fee of 4.5% of any pre-IPO funds raised, and 6% of Tendcare’s pre-IPO undiluted shares would be issued in equal proportions to three officers of Hui Xiang Group Pte Ltd.

On 2013-09-19, a Letter of Engagement between Beijing Tianjian Huaxia Medical Investment Management Co Ltd and Hui Xiang Group Pte Ltd was signed by Gong Ruizhong and Ryan Gwee, setting out terms for Hui Xiang Group Pte Ltd to advise on pre-IPO restructuring and assist in pre-IPO fundraising and preparations for the Tendcare initial public offering.

On 2014-06-02, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd entered into a share purchase agreement with NYC Investments Limited and Luxe Heritage Capital Management Limited under which NYC Investments Limited would subscribe for 441 new shares and Luxe Heritage Capital Management Limited would subscribe for 882 new shares in Tendcare at a stated price of US$27,540.89 per share.

In 2014, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd, Luxe Heritage Capital Management Limited, and NYC Investments Limited entered into a supplemental agreement reducing the price per share under their earlier share purchase agreement from US$27,540.89 to US$14,603.17 and reducing the total consideration to US$19,320,000.

In 2014, an undated memorandum of understanding was entered into among Gong Ruizhong, Ryan Gwee, and Sim Mong Teck stating that shares issued to NYC Investments Limited and Luxe Heritage Capital Management Limited in Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd were intended to be provided without payment in consideration for services rendered to Beijing Tianjian Huaxia Medical Investment Management Co Ltd and its intended restructured group.

On 2014-06-13, following receipt of funds from Atlantis-related entities, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd transferred US$5,000,000 to Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd while Gong Ruizhong was director of both companies.
On 2015-02-06, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd transferred US$15,000,000 to Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd while both companies were under the control of Gong Ruizhong.
On 2015-02-09, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd transferred US$5,000,000 to Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd.
On 2015-09-23, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd transferred S$250,000 to Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd.
On 2015-09-25, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd transferred US$2,000,000 to Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd.
On 2015-10-26, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd transferred US$1,000,000 to Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd.
On 2015-11-13, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd transferred US$3,000,000 to Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd.
On 2015-11-16, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd transferred S$250,000 to Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd.
On 2015-12-08, Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd transferred US$4,000,000 to Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd.

On 2015-01-26, an agreement referred to as the Termination Agreement was signed by Gong Ruizhong, Miao Weiguo, Ryan Gwee, and Sim Mong Teck, providing for termination of earlier arrangements including a 2013 memorandum of understanding and a 2014 memorandum of understanding, and for the transfer of rights and interests in NYC Investments Limited and Luxe Heritage Capital Management Limited to Gongs Global Investment Development Holdings Limited.

On 2015-02-01, a Success Fee Agreement was entered into between Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd and Hui Xiang Group Pte Ltd under which Hui Xiang Group Pte Ltd would assist in pre-IPO fund raising in return for a success fee of 5.5% of pre-IPO funding raised.

On 2015-02-01, an IPO Shares Agreement was entered into under which Gongs Global Investment Development Holdings Limited agreed to issue 3% of the undiluted ordinary shares in Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd to either Qian Hui Capital Limited or Miao Weiguo in connection with assistance on reorganisation of Tendcare’s medical business for the proposed initial public offering.

On 2015-02-01, a Retainer Agreement was entered into under which Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd agreed to pay Qian Hui Capital Limited a monthly retainer fee of HK$300,000 until Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd was listed, in connection with assistance on reorganisation for the proposed initial public offering.
On 2017-06-02, 99% of the shares in Shanxi Jinbang Energy Technology Group Co Ltd that had been held by Gong Ruizhong were transferred to the third defendant.

On 2017-06-29, Shanxi Tian Jian Hua Xia Business Trading Co Ltd transferred all of its shares in Beijing Tianjian Huaxia Medical Investment Management Co Ltd to Shanxi Jinbang Energy Technology Group Co Ltd, a company that was not part of the Tian Jian Group.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the director and controlling shareholder of Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd and Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd was personally responsible for company debts on the basis that Tendcare’s business was carried on with intent to defraud creditors or for a fraudulent purpose, and whether he is responsible for losses arising from specific fund transfers.
Party_Details.Rule: If a company’s business is carried on with intent to defraud creditors or for a fraudulent purpose, any person who is knowingly a party to the carrying on of the business in that manner may be declared personally responsible, without limitation, for all or any of the company’s debts or liabilities. A director who authorises or directs the use of company funds in such circumstances may also be restore misapplied sums or compensate the company for resulting losses.
Party_Details.Application: Gong Ruizhong incorporated and controlled Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd and Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd, and he signed key agreements relating to the proposed initial public offering and pre-IPO fundraising. Tendcare received substantial funds from institutional investors and lenders and, within short intervals, transferred large amounts to Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd, Tian Jian Hua Xia Medical Group (HK) Limited, and Hui Xiang Group (HK) Limited. Gong Ruizhong did not provide contemporaneous documentation showing that these transfers were applied to the Tian Jian Group’s operations or to expenses of the proposed listing. The court examined his pleaded reliance on an Entrustment Agreement and on internal schedules purporting to show downstream investments, but it did not accept these materials as reliable proof of legitimate application of the funds. In light of his control over the sending and receiving companies and the absence of a credible explanation or documentary trail, the court treated him as a knowing participant in the way Tendcare’s business was carried on and allocated personal responsibility for specified debts and misapplied sums.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court held that Tendcare’s business had been carried on for a fraudulent purpose in relation to certain debts and that Gong Ruizhong was the directing mind behind the relevant conduct. It noted that he controlled both Tendcare and Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd, authorised or caused the disputed transfers, and failed to attend trial for cross-examination. Applying Order 38 rule 2(1) of the Rules of Court, the judge declined to admit his affidavit evidence and assessed his defence based on the remaining record. The court rejected the authenticity and probative value of the purported Entrustment Agreement, observing that the original was not produced, its reproduction was inadequately explained, and there was no clear commercial reason for routing funds through Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd when both companies were under his control. The judge found no satisfactory evidence that the disputed transfers were used for the Tian Jian Group’s capital investments, working capital, or IPO expenses, and drew an adverse inference from the absence of bank records tracing the funds beyond accounts associated with Gong or entities he controlled. On this basis, the court declared that Gong was knowingly a party to the fraudulent carrying on of Tendcare’s business and was personally responsible for specified debts, and it also addressed his liability under other heads of claim, including directors’ duties and deceit, in light of the same factual matrix.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd was incorporated in Singapore on 2013-07-15 as an exempt private company.
From its incorporation on 2013-07-15, Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd carried on business as a commission agent.
From 2013-07-15, Gong Ruizhong was the sole director and shareholder of Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd, holding 2,500,001 fully paid-up shares.
On 2014-06-13, Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd received US$5,000,000 from Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd.
On 2015-02-06, Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd received US$15,000,000 from Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd.
On 2015-02-09, Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd received US$5,000,000 from Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd.
On 2015-09-23, Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd received S$250,000 from Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd.
On 2015-09-25, Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd received US$2,000,000 from Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd.
On 2015-10-26, Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd received US$1,000,000 from Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd.
On 2015-11-13, Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd received US$3,000,000 from Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd.
On 2015-11-16, Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd received S$250,000 from Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd.
On 2015-12-08, Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd received US$4,000,000 from Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd.

A document dated 2014-03-03, described as an Entrustment Agreement, stated that Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd would make payments for and on behalf of Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd, although the original document was not produced in later proceedings.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd, as a company controlled by the same individual who controlled Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd, is personally responsible for Tendcare’s debts and for sums transferred from Tendcare, on the basis that it participated in the carrying on of Tendcare’s business for a fraudulent purpose.
Party_Details.Rule: A company that receives funds from another company and is controlled by the same individual may be treated as a party to fraudulent trading if it is used as a vehicle in the carrying on of the other company’s business with intent to defraud creditors or for a fraudulent purpose, and if the controlling individual’s knowledge and conduct are attributable to it.
Party_Details.Application: Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd was wholly owned and directed by Gong Ruizhong and received multiple large transfers in US dollars and Singapore dollars from Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd shortly after Tendcare obtained funds from institutional investors and lenders. The company’s role under an Entrustment Agreement was not supported by production of the original document or by a clear commercial rationale for routing funds between two Singapore companies under common control. The court attributed Gong Ruizhong’s knowledge and conduct to Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd and treated the company as a participant in the pattern of transfers that formed part of the fraudulent carrying on of Tendcare’s business.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court accepted that Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd was effectively the alter ego of Gong Ruizhong, given that he was its sole director and shareholder and executed all relevant documents on its behalf. It found that the company received substantial sums from Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd without adequate evidence that these funds were applied to the Tian Jian Group’s operations or IPO expenses. The judge rejected the Entrustment Agreement as unreliable, noting the absence of the original and the lack of a convincing explanation for its reproduction. In light of the attribution of Gong’s knowledge to Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd, the court concluded that the company was knowingly a party to the fraudulent carrying on of Tendcare’s business and declared it personally responsible for specified debts and transfers, while also addressing its counterclaim for reimbursement of alleged disbursements made on Tendcare’s behalf.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Miao Weiguo

Facts.Text: Hui Xiang Group Pte Ltd was incorporated in Singapore on 2011-03-30 and engaged in management consultancy.
From 2011-03-30, Miao Weiguo was the sole director and shareholder of Hui Xiang Group Pte Ltd.
Qian Hui Capital Limited was incorporated in Hong Kong on 2005-01-14 and was described by Miao Weiguo as the private investment arm of Hui Xiang Group Pte Ltd.
From 2005-01-14, Miao Weiguo was the sole director and shareholder of Qian Hui Capital Limited.
Imperium Mining Company was incorporated in the Cayman Islands on 2012-02-08.
From 2012-02-08, Miao Weiguo was the sole director of Imperium Mining Company.
Hui Xiang Group (HK) Limited was a Hong Kong-incorporated company that was wholly owned by Hui Xiang Group Pte Ltd until 2015-03-31.
Throughout the material period, Miao Weiguo was the sole director of Hui Xiang Group (HK) Limited.
On 2015-03-31, Hui Xiang Group (HK) Limited became a wholly-owned subsidiary of Imperium Mining Company, which was controlled by Miao Weiguo.

On 2013-09-03, Hui Xiang Group Pte Ltd entered into a memorandum of understanding with Beijing Tianjian Huaxia Medical Investment Management Co Ltd regarding advisory and fundraising work for a proposed initial public offering of Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd.

On 2013-09-19, a Letter of Engagement between Beijing Tianjian Huaxia Medical Investment Management Co Ltd and Hui Xiang Group Pte Ltd was signed, under which Hui Xiang Group Pte Ltd was to advise on pre-IPO restructuring and assist in pre-IPO fundraising and preparations for the Tendcare initial public offering.

From late 2013 into 2014, Hui Xiang Group Pte Ltd, through its officers including Ryan Gwee and Sim Mong Teck, worked with KPMG and MCL Capital Limited on pre-IPO restructuring, fundraising, and project administration for the proposed Tendcare initial public offering.

On 2014-12-15, a shareholder’s resolution of Hui Xiang Group Pte Ltd recorded the dismissal of Ryan Gwee as Chief Executive Officer and the termination of the services of Sim Mong Teck with immediate effect.
On 2014-12-17, Hui Xiang Group Pte Ltd and Beijing Tianjian Huaxia Medical Investment Management Co Ltd signed a termination agreement ending the 2013 Letter of Engagement between them.

On 2015-01-26, an agreement referred to as the Termination Agreement was signed by Miao Weiguo, Gong Ruizhong, Ryan Gwee, and Sim Mong Teck, terminating earlier arrangements including a 2013 memorandum of understanding and a 2014 memorandum of understanding and providing for the transfer of rights and interests in NYC Investments Limited and Luxe Heritage Capital Management Limited to Gongs Global Investment Development Holdings Limited.

On 2015-02-01, Hui Xiang Group Pte Ltd and Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd entered into a Success Fee Agreement under which Hui Xiang Group Pte Ltd would receive a success fee of 5.5% of pre-IPO funding raised in return for assistance on pre-IPO fundraising.

On 2015-02-01, an IPO Shares Agreement was entered into under which Gongs Global Investment Development Holdings Limited agreed to issue 3% of the undiluted ordinary shares in Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd to either Qian Hui Capital Limited or Miao Weiguo in connection with assistance on reorganisation of Tendcare’s medical business for the proposed initial public offering.

On 2015-02-01, a Retainer Agreement was entered into under which Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd agreed to pay Qian Hui Capital Limited a monthly retainer fee of HK$300,000 until Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd was listed, in connection with assistance on reorganisation for the proposed initial public offering.
On 2015-02-27, Hui Xiang Group (HK) Limited received US$1,750,000 from Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd as part of payments described as success fees.
On 2015-04-10, Hui Xiang Group (HK) Limited received US$2,540,000 from Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd as part of payments described as success fees.

On 2015-04-14, a document described as a loan agreement was dated between Qian Hui Capital Limited and Tian Jian Hua Xia Medical Group (HK) Limited in relation to US$2,000,000 that had been transferred from Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd to Tian Jian Hua Xia Medical Group (HK) Limited on 2015-03-11.

On 2015-09-22, a document described as a loan agreement was dated between Qian Hui Capital Limited and Tian Jian Hua Xia Medical Group (HK) Limited in relation to US$4,000,000 that had been transferred from Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd to Tian Jian Hua Xia Medical Group (HK) Limited on 2015-09-22.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the individual who controlled Hui Xiang Group Pte Ltd, Hui Xiang Group (HK) Limited, and Qian Hui Capital Limited participated in the carrying on of Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd’s business for a fraudulent purpose, and whether he and his companies are responsible for sums received from Tendcare.
Party_Details.Rule: A person who controls advisory or related entities involved in a company’s fundraising and restructuring may be held personally responsible for the company’s debts under fraudulent trading provisions if he knowingly participates in the carrying on of the company’s business with intent to defraud creditors or for a fraudulent purpose. Separate from fraudulent trading, a person or entity that receives company funds under arrangements that do not reflect genuine commercial transactions may be exposed to liability under doctrines addressing misapplied funds.
Party_Details.Application: Miao Weiguo, through Hui Xiang Group Pte Ltd, entered into early agreements to advise on and support the proposed Tendcare initial public offering, including a memorandum of understanding and a Letter of Engagement with Beijing Tianjian Huaxia Medical Investment Management Co Ltd. He later entered into the Termination Agreement and the Post-Termination Agreements, which provided for a success fee, an equity allocation in Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd, and a monthly retainer payable to Qian Hui Capital Limited. Hui Xiang Group (HK) Limited received US$1,750,000 and US$2,540,000 from Tendcare as part of payments described as success fees, and Qian Hui Capital Limited was named as lender in documents dated 2015-04-14 and 2015-09-22 relating to US$2,000,000 and US$4,000,000 that had been transferred from Tendcare to Tian Jian Hua Xia Medical Group (HK) Limited. The court examined whether these transactions showed that Miao Weiguo was a co-architect of a broader scheme to misuse Tendcare’s funds or whether they reflected advisory and fundraising work for the proposed listing. It concluded that, although certain loan documents involving Qian Hui Capital Limited did not reflect genuine loans and raised concerns about the use of funds, the evidence did not establish that Miao Weiguo was a knowing participant in an overarching fraudulent scheme to use Tendcare as a vehicle to defraud creditors. The court therefore did not impose fraudulent trading liability on him or his companies, while recognising that the sham nature of some loan arrangements was other heads of claim.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court considered the plaintiffs’ contention that Miao Weiguo was a co-conspirator with Gong Ruizhong in a scheme to misuse pre-IPO funds raised by Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd. It reviewed evidence of Miao’s close personal relationship with Gong, his control of Hui Xiang Group Pte Ltd, Hui Xiang Group (HK) Limited, and Qian Hui Capital Limited, and his involvement in the Tendcare IPO advisory structure. The judge accepted that Miao was kept informed of the IPO process and that Hui Xiang Group Pte Ltd played a real role in fundraising and restructuring, supported by correspondence from KPMG and other advisers. The court found that the success fee arrangements had a commercial basis and that the payments to Hui Xiang Group (HK) Limited could be understood as part payment of agreed success fees. It also noted that the Post-Termination Agreements increased Miao’s prospective equity interest in Tendcare, which would have been of limited value if the intention had been to strip the company of assets. However, the court found that the loan agreements involving Qian Hui Capital Limited and Tian Jian Hua Xia Medical Group (HK) Limited did not reflect genuine loans and that Miao’s explanation that they were used to circumvent fund transfer restrictions was not convincing. The judge treated this as evidence of dishonesty relevant to claims such as dishonest assistance and knowing receipt but concluded that it did not prove that Miao was a principal in a comprehensive fraudulent trading scheme. Accordingly, the court declined to hold Miao and his companies liable for fraudulent trading, while leaving open their exposure under other causes of action.
